page: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v6 is an Esv6-family virtual machine configured with 32 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector and matrix extensions such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions). This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 246.92 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 141.39 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Compute performance is highlighted by strong single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores of 2143.0 and 17375.0, respectively. In data processing tasks, the instance achieves a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.38 GB/sec and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 199.28 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds of 178.81 tokens/sec on medium-sized 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32s_v6 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its 32 vCPUs, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints without the expense of scaling up to higher vCPU tiers. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on memory capacity and CPU execution. This profile is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, including relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, featuring 32 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance utilizes the x86_64 architecture and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage compatibility. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-core decompression, and medium-model LLM prompt processing. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as large databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, designed for memory-intensive database and analytics workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32s_v6 is an Esv6-family virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring advanced Intel Xeon processing and exceptional memory bandwidth for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 512.0 GB RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core ratio
Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with AMX and AVX-512 support
Strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 246.92 GB/sec
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
